data.title: Hick’s Law
data.description: Simplifies navigation to reduce decision-making time for users.
data.technical_spec: Menus and choices are optimized to prevent user overload.

data.title: Fitts’s Law
data.description: Designs for touch targets are optimized to improve accessibility.
data.technical_spec: Interactive elements are suitably sized and positioned for quick access.

data.title: Miller’s Law
data.description: Limits the amount of content displayed to enhance user memory retention.
data.technical_spec: The amount of information presented per screen is capped to prevent cognitive overload.

data.title: Aesthetic-Usability Effect
data.description: Visual design is used to increase user perception of usability.
data.technical_spec: The interface uses consistent color schemes and typography for a pleasing aesthetic.

data.title: Jakob’s Law
data.description: Familiar design patterns are used to leverage users’ existing knowledge.
data.technical_spec: Navigation and structure resemble other sites users frequent.
